origin.name: Cameroon
origin.key_document: Acte de deces (death certificate, may be in French or English depending on region)
origin.source: FCDO Cameroon travel advice, updated 2025; British High Commission Yaounde guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Cameroon is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option alongside full body repatriation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Acte de deces (certified English translation required if in French)
special_rules: Cameroon has both French-speaking and English-speaking regions; documentation language varies.
French-language documentation requires certified English translation for UK acceptance.
Police clearance is required for all violent or suspicious deaths.
An export permit from Cameroonian authorities is required for all repatriations.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All non-English documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British High Commission in Yaounde can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Cameroon to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Cameroon. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-4 weeks. Civil registry, French translation, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
